title: pelargonium × gordoniae
intro: Pelargonium × gordoniae, also known as Cape Geranium or Mountain Rose, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, native to the southwestern region of South Africa. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits associated with Pelargonium × gordoniae include relief from cold and flu symptoms, sore throats, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and extracts, are often used to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
